name: io.github.yambr/open-computer-use
title: Open Computer Use
description: Self-hosted MCP server that gives any LLM an isolated Ubuntu sandbox with bash, file editing, browser automation (Playwright), document creation (docx/xlsx/pptx/pdf), and Claude Code sub-agent. Each chat session gets its own Docker container. Deploy with docker compose, then connect any MCP client via Streamable HTTP.

server_url.description: URL of your deployed Open Computer Use server

headers.name: Authorization
headers.description: Bearer token for MCP API authentication (value of MCP_API_KEY env var on server)

headers.name: X-Chat-Id
headers.description: Session identifier — each unique ID gets its own isolated sandbox container

headers.name: X-User-Email
headers.description: User email for per-user skills and token lookup

environmentVariables.name: MCP_API_KEY
environmentVariables.description: API key for authenticating MCP requests. Leave empty for development (no auth).

environmentVariables.name: CONTAINER_IDLE_TIMEOUT
environmentVariables.description: Auto-stop idle containers after N seconds

environmentVariables.name: ENABLE_NETWORK
environmentVariables.description: Allow sandbox containers to access the network

environmentVariables.name: SINGLE_USER_MODE
environmentVariables.description: Set to 'true' for single-user setup (no X-Chat-Id needed), 'false' to require X-Chat-Id (multi-user). Unset = lenient (works without chat ID but shows a warning)
